title: Avoid Testing Exception Types Using 'instanceof' in Catch Blocks
description: This rule discourages the use of 'instanceof' to test exception types in catch blocks. In Java, the catch block is designed to handle specific types of exceptions. Using 'instanceof' to manually check the exception type is unnecessary as Java automatically matches the exception type to the appropriate catch block based on the declared exception type in the catch clauses.
recommendation: Let Java handle the exception type matching automatically. Refrain from using 'instanceof' in catch blocks to manually check the exception type.
impact: Using 'instanceof' in catch blocks for exception type testing can lead to unnecessary complexity in the code, making it harder to read and maintain. It also goes against Java's automatic exception type matching in catch clauses, which could lead to potential bugs.

checks.message: Avoid testing exception types using 'instanceof' in catch blocks
